statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: A list of integers with a shift applied
statement.outputFormat: Whether the array is stable (equal) before and after the shift

examples.explanation: The array is a rotation of itself

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { return true; } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums) { return true; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { vector<int> pre = nums; sort(pre.begin(), pre.end()); vector<int> post(nums.size()); for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { post[(nums.size() - 1 - i) % nums.size()] = nums[i]; } sort(post.begin(), post.end()); return pre == post; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums) { int[] pre = nums.clone(); Arrays.sort(pre); int[] post = new int[nums.length]; for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { post[(nums.length - 1 - i) % nums.length] = nums[i]; } Arrays.sort(post); return Arrays.equals(pre, post); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: pre = nums.copy(); pre.sort(); post = nums[:] for i in range(len(nums)): post[(len(nums) - 1 - i) % len(nums)] = nums[i]; post.sort(); return pre == post
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { let pre = nums.slice(); pre.sort(function(a, b) {return a-b}); let post = nums.slice(); for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { post[(nums.length - 1 - i) % nums.length] = nums[i]; } post.sort(function(a, b) {return a-b}); return JSON.stringify(pre) === JSON.stringify(post);}

solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: pre = nums[:]; pre.sort(); post = nums[:] for i in range(len(nums)): post[(i + 1) % len(nums)] = nums[i]; post.sort(); return pre == post
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> #include <vector> #include <algorithm> #include <string> using namespace std; int main() { int n; cin >> n; vector<int> nums(n); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { cin >> nums[i]; } vector<int> pre = nums; sort(pre.begin(), pre.end()); pre.resize(n); vector<int> post(nums.size()); for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { post[(i + 1) % nums.size()] = nums[i]; } sort(post.begin(), post.end()); post.resize(n); if (pre == post) { cout << "True"; } else { cout << "False"; } cout <<endl; return 0; }
